title: Erigeron litoralis
taxonomy: Erigeron litoralis is commonly known as beach fleabane. It is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is native to western North America, where it grows in coastal areas, beaches, and dunes. Erigeron litoralis is a dainty annual or perennial herb that produces small white or pinkish flowers in late spring and early summer. As a member of the daisy family, Erigeron litoralis is closely related to other species of Erigeron and other genera within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Erigeron litoralis, also known as beach daisy, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and flowers have been employed to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with colds and sore throats. Additionally, Erigeron litoralis has been used to treat skin conditions, including minor wounds and rashes. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Erigeron litoralis for medicinal purposes.

These constituents include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.
